summary.text:
Pattern class.
The core building block of Satori. Patterns represent time-varying values over cycles.
Add a value or pattern.
Compare values. If both are true, return 1, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
coin().and(coin()) // returns 1 when both coins() are true
```

summary.text: Assuming an array, take the element at the given index.

content.text:
```ts
'Dmi'.at(2) // returns A
```

```ts
'Dmi'.at([0,2]) // returns D and A
```

summary.text: born - like changed, but return 1 only when the value changes from falsy to truthy, else 0.
Return the current cycle as an integer.

content.text:
```ts
c() // current cycle
```

```ts
c(4) // current cycle modulo 4
```

summary.text: Run cellular automata on a pattern. Just handles Game of Life for now, but could be expanded to other rules.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4) // runs Game of Life on a 4x4 grid, with a random initial state. Returns an array of 16 values representing the grid state each cycle.
```
a 1D array representing the grid state, where each cell is either 0 (dead) or 1 (alive).

summary.text: Fill a cache with n number of cycles worth of values from the preceding pattern

content.text:
```ts
'1?0*8'.cache(1,every(4)) // caches 1 cycle worth of random 0 or 1s. Clears the cache every 4 cycles.
```

summary.text: Concat values, one per cycle.

content.text:
```ts
cat('A', 'B', 'C') // A for 1 cycle, B for 1 cycle, C for 1 cycle.
```

summary.text: changed - 1 if an item changed, 0 if remains the same. If recieves a single value, compares it to the previous value and returns a single value. If recieves an array, compares each item to the previous value at the same index and returns an array of 1s and 0s.

content.text:
```ts
choose(0,1,2).changed() // returns 1 when the value changes, 0 when it stays the same
```

```ts
ca(3).row(0).changed() // returns an array of 3 values containing 1s and 0s
```

summary.text: Randomly choose from a set of values.

content.text:
```ts
choose('A', 'B', 'C') // randomly chooses A, B, or C each cycle.
```

summary.text: Clamp pattern values to a given range.

content.text:
```ts
random().mul(10).clamp(2, 4) // clamps random()*10 values to between 2 and 4
```

summary.text: Return an equal distribution of 1s and 0s.

content.text:
```ts
coin() // returns 0 or 1 with equal probability
```

```ts
coin('A', 'B') // returns 'A' or 'B' with equal probability
```

```ts
(60).add(coin(5)) // adds either 0 or 5 to 60 with equal probability
```

```ts
(60).add((5).coin()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return all values in the given column.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4).col(0) // returns the first column of the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

```ts
ca(4).col(count()) // returns a different column each event, cycling through the columns as count() increments
```

summary.text: Combine patterns into one array of values

content.text:
```ts
combine('C E G') // returns ['C', 'E', 'G']
```

```ts
'C E G'.combine('D F A') // returns ['C', 'D', 'E', 'F', 'G', 'A']
```

summary.text: Generate a cosine wave from min to max over one cycle

content.text:
```ts
cosine(0, 1) // a cosine wave from 0 to 1 over the course of 1 cycle
```

summary.text: Increment a counter each time a condition is met, if no condition is provided, increments every time it is called.

content.text:
```ts
coin().count() // increments the count each time coin() returns 1
```

```ts
count(coin()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Return the current cycles per second.

Convert cycles to hertz. E.g. with a cps of 1 (1 second cycles), 
cthz(1/4) would return 4, since a quarter cycle corresponds to a frequency of 4 cycles per second.
With a cps of 2 (half second cycles), 
cthz(1/4) would return 2, since a quarter cycle corresponds to a frequency of 2 cycles per second.
Cycles to milliseconds.

content.text:
```ts
(4).ctms() // convert a number as a method
```

```ts
ctms(1) // or as a function
```

```ts
'1 2 3 4'.ctms() // convert a mini pattern string
```

```ts
seq(1,2,3,4).ctms() // convert a Pattern
```

summary.text: Convert cycles to seconds.

content.text:
```ts
(4).cts() // convert a number as a method
```

```ts
cts(1) // or as a function
```

```ts
'1 2 3 4'.cts() // convert a mini pattern string
```

```ts
seq(1,2,3,4).cts() // convert a Pattern
```

summary.text: Randomly replace values with 0.

content.text:
```ts
sine().degrade(0.3) // randomly replaces 30% of sine wave values with 0
```

summary.text: Assuming an array of values, return the number of values that are above a certain threshold, as a normalised value between 0 and 1.

content.text:
```ts
ca(16).density(0.5) // returns a normalised count of how many cells in the 4x4 Game of Life grid are above 0.5
```

```ts
ca(4).row(0).density(0.5) // returns a normalised count of how many cells in the first row of the Game of Life grid are above 0.5
```

summary.text:
Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return all values at the given diagonal
top-left = 0 to bottom-right = size-1. bottom-left = -size to top-right = size*2-1
died - like changed, but return 1 only when the value changes from truthy to falsy, else 0.
Divide a value or pattern.
Equal to comparison. Returns 1 if value == threshold, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
seq(1,2,3).eq(saw(0,4,3)) // compares each value with saw values
```

summary.text: Return a 1 every n cycles, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
every(0.25) // returns 1 every quarter cycle. Equivalent to seq(1,1,1,1).
```

```ts
every(2) // returns 1 every 2 cycles. Equivalent to seq(1).slow(2).
```

summary.text: Expand the current value into a list of n values. An optional function can be provided to transform each value.

content.text:
```ts
'C'.expand(3) // returns stack('C', 'C', 'C')
```

```ts
'C'.expand(3, (note, i) => note + i * 2) // returns stack('C', 'D', 'E')
```

```ts
s1.set({
   inst: 'synth', n: 'C4*2'.expand(16, (x,i) => x+i*2),
   strum: ctms(1/8),
   e: every(2) })
```

summary.text: Speed up a pattern.

content.text:
```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C').fast(3) // A for 1/3 cycle, B for 1/3 cycle, C for 1/3 cycle.
```

summary.text: To fixed decimal places.
Use a custom function to transform pattern values.
Frequency to MIDI note number.
Greater than comparison. Returns 1 if value > threshold, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
seq(1,2,3).gt(saw(0,4,3)) // compares each value with saw values
```

summary.text:
Sample and hold a pattern at n cycles per sample.
Samples the pattern once per n cycles and holds the value for that duration.

content.text:
```ts
random().hold(2) // new random value every 2 cycles
```

```ts
coin().hold(0.5) // new coin toss every half cycle (2 per cycle)
```

summary.text: Hold the last value of a pattern until a condition is met, then update the value.

content.text:
```ts
'C E G'.holdUntil(coin()) // holds the current chord until coin() returns 1, then updates to the next chord in the sequence
```

summary.text: Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return values of the 8 neighbouring cells for the cell at the given index. Neighbours are returned in the order: top-left, top, top-right, left, right, bottom-left, bottom, bottom-right. If a neighbour is out of bounds, it will wrap around to the opposite side of the grid.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4).hood(0,0) // returns the 8 neighbors of the cell at coordinates 0,0 in the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

summary.text: Alias for ifelse.
If else control structure for patterns.

content.text:
```ts
coin().ifelse('A', 'B') // returns 'A' when coin() is true, else 'B'
```

```ts
ifelse('A', 'B', coin()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Assuming an array, return 1 if includes the given value, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
'C E G'.includes('E') // returns 1
```

```ts
'C E G'.includes('D') // returns 0
```

summary.text: Assuming an array, return the indexes of the elements that match the given value.

content.text:
```ts
'C E G'.indexesOf('E') // returns [1]
```

```ts
'C E G'.indexesOf('D') // returns []
```

summary.text: Interpolate values.

content.text:
```ts
sine().interp(saw()) // interpolates between sine and saw waveforms over time
```

summary.text: Assuming a chord pattern, invert the chord by a given number of steps.

content.text:
```ts
'Cma#7'.invert(1) // returns 'E G B C'
```

```ts
'Cma#7'.invert(-1) // returns 'B C E G'
```

summary.text: Asssuming an array, join together into a string with an optional separator.

content.text:
```ts
'C E G'.join() // returns 'CEG'
```

```ts
'C E G'.join(',') // returns 'C,E,G'
```

summary.text:
Listen to a MIDI input device, on a channel. 
Record notes and loop them.
Recorded notes persist across code evaluations until cleared.

additional arguments for loop control -
cycles: number or pattern - length of the loop in cycles. Default is 1.
record: boolean or pattern - whether to record incoming notes. Default is true.
clear: boolean or pattern - whether to clear recorded notes. Default is false.
Less than comparison. Returns 1 if value < threshold, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
seq(1,2,3).lt(saw(0,4,3)) // compares each value with saw values
```

summary.text: Get the current audio level from a Stream.
Parse a mini pattern string into a Pattern instance.

content.text:
```ts
mini('Cmaj7..?') // parses the mini pattern string into a Pattern
```

summary.text: Modulo by a value or pattern.
MIDI note number to frequency.
Map from one range to another.
Multiply a value or pattern.
Not equal to comparison. Returns 1 if value != threshold, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
seq(1,2,3).neq(saw(0,4,3)) // compares each value with saw values
```

summary.text: Noise waveform from min to max over one cycle.

content.text:
```ts
noise() // noise values between 0 and 1
```

```ts
noise(5, 10) // noise values between 5 and 10
```

```ts
noise().slow(3) // noise values between 0 and 1, slowed down over 3 cycles
```

summary.text: If a value is true, negate it (1 -> 0, 0 -> 1).

content.text:
```ts
coin().not() // returns 1 when coin() is 0, else 0
```

```ts
not(coin()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Return mostly 1s, occasionally 0s.

content.text:
```ts
often()
```

```ts
often('A', 'B') // returns 'A' when condition is true, else 'B'
```

```ts
(60).add(often(5)) // adds 5 to 60 most of the time
```

```ts
(60).add((5).often()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Return a 1 at the earliest convenience, then 0s after that.
Compare values. If one of them is true, return 1, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
coin().or(coin()) // returns 1 when either coin() is true
```

summary.text: Print the current Pattern value for debugging.

content.text:
```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C').print() // prints the value of the pattern on each division of the cycle.
```

```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C').print('MyPattern') // prints the value of the pattern with an identifier.
```

summary.text: Generate a pulse wave from min to max over one cycle.

content.text:
```ts
pulse(0, 1, 0.25) // a pulse wave from 0 to 1 with a duty cycle of 25% over the course of 1 cycle
```

summary.text: Alias for qmeasure.
Measure Qubit at index.

content.text:
```ts
qm(0) // measures qubit 0
```

summary.text: Measure all Qubits. Returns an array of measured values.

content.text:
```ts
qmeasures() // measures all qubits
```

summary.text: Alias for qmeasures.
Alias for qphase.
Phase of basis state at index.

content.text:
```ts
qphase(0) // phase of basis state 0
```

summary.text: Phases of all basis states. As an array.

content.text:
```ts
qphases() // phases of all basis states
```

summary.text: Alias for qphases.
Alias for qprob.
Probability of basis state at index.

content.text:
```ts
qprob(0) // probability of basis state 0
```

summary.text: Probabilities of all basis states. As an array.

content.text:
```ts
qprobs() // probabilities of all basis states
```

summary.text: Alias for qprobs.
Alias for saw
Alias for saw
Return mostly 0s, occasionally 1s. Optionally, provide if-else patterns.

content.text:
```ts
rarely()
```

```ts
rarely('A', 'B') // returns 'A' when condition is true, else 'B'
```

```ts
(60).add(rarely(5)) // adds 5 to 60 occasionally
```

```ts
(60).add((5).rarely()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return values from x1,y1 to x2,y2. If x2 or y2 are out of bounds, they will wrap around to the beginning of the row or column.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4).region(0,0,2,2) // returns the top-left 2x2 region of the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

summary.text: Reset a pattern's internal time when a condition is truthy.

content.text:
```ts
sine(0,1).reset(every(0.5)) // sine wave that restarts every half cycle
```

```ts
saw(0,1).reset(coin()) // saw wave that randomly resets
```

summary.text: Retrieve data from the store

Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return values in concentric rings from the centre outwards. The first ring (0) is the centre cell, the second ring (1) is the 8 neighbours, the third ring (2) is the 16 cells surrounding those, etc.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4).ring(0) // returns the centre cell of the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

```ts
ca(4).ring(1) // returns the 8 neighbours of the centre cell in the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

summary.text: Shift a pattern in time by n cycles.

content.text:
```ts
sine().rotate(0.5) // sine wave 0.5 cycles out of phase
```

```ts
seq(1,0,1,0).rotate(0.25) // shift sequence by a quarter cycle
```

summary.text: Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return all values in the given row.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4).row(0) // returns the first row of the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

```ts
ca(4).row(count()) // returns a different row each event, cycling through the rows as count() increments
```

summary.text: Generate a ramp from min to max, once per cycle.

content.text:
```ts
saw(0,4) // a ramp from 0 to 4 over the course of 1 cycle
```

```ts
saw(0,1,96).slow(2) // a ramp from 0 to 1 over the course of 2 cycles, with finer steps to mitigate the slow pattern
```

summary.text: Alias for mtr.
Sequence values into a single cycle.

content.text:
```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C', 'D') // A for .25 cycle, B for .25 cycle ... D for .25 cycle
```

summary.text: Alias for cat.
Generate a sine wave from min to max over one cycle.

content.text:
```ts
sine(0, 1) // a sine wave from 0 to 1 over the course of 1 cycle
```

```ts
sine(-1, 1, 96).slow(2) // a sine wave from -1 to 1 over the course of 2 cycles, with finer steps to mitigate the slow pattern
```

summary.text: Slow down a pattern.

content.text:
```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C').slow(2) // A for 2 cycles, B for 2 cycles, C for 2 cycles.
```

summary.text: Alias for coin
Alias for square
Alias for pulse with a duty cycle of 0.5.

content.text:
```ts
square(0, 4) // a square wave from 0 to 4 over the course of 1 cycle
```

summary.text: Layer values over the same time range.

content.text:
```ts
stack(0, sine(), square()) // layers a sine wave and square wave over a constant 0 value.
```

summary.text: Round to a particular step.

content.text:
```ts
random().round(0.25) // random values rounded to nearest 0.25
```

summary.text: Subtract a value or pattern.
Return the current time in cycles.

content.text:
```ts
t() // current time e.g. 3.25 (3 cycles and a quarter)
```

```ts
t(4) // current time modulo 4
```

summary.text:
Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return values of the specified tile.
Tiles are indexed starting from the top-left, going row by row. For example, in a 4x4 grid, tile(2,2) would return the bottom-right 2x2 section.
Toggle 1s and 0s when the condition is met.

content.text:
```ts
rarely().toggle() // toggles between 1 and 0 each time rarely() returns 1
```

summary.text: Generate a triangle wave from min to max over one cycle

content.text:
```ts
tri(0, 1) // a triangle wave from 0 to 1 over the course of 1 cycle
```

summary.text: Compare values. If one is true and the other is false, return 1, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
set(1).xor(1) // returns 0
```

summary.text: Add a value or pattern.
Compare values. If both are true, return 1, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
coin().and(coin()) // returns 1 when both coins() are true
```

summary.text: Assuming an array, take the element at the given index.

content.text:
```ts
'Dmi'.at(2) // returns A
```

```ts
'Dmi'.at([0,2]) // returns D and A
```

summary.text: If else control structure for patterns.

content.text:
```ts
coin().ifelse('A', 'B') // returns 'A' when coin() is true, else 'B'
```

```ts
ifelse('A', 'B', coin()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Assuming an array, return 1 if includes the given value, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
'C E G'.includes('E') // returns 1
```

```ts
'C E G'.includes('D') // returns 0
```

summary.text: Assuming an array, return the indexes of the elements that match the given value.

content.text:
```ts
'C E G'.indexesOf('E') // returns [1]
```

```ts
'C E G'.indexesOf('D') // returns []
```

summary.text: Interpolate values.

content.text:
```ts
sine().interp(saw()) // interpolates between sine and saw waveforms over time
```

summary.text: Assuming a chord pattern, invert the chord by a given number of steps.

content.text:
```ts
'Cma#7'.invert(1) // returns 'E G B C'
```

```ts
'Cma#7'.invert(-1) // returns 'B C E G'
```

summary.text: Asssuming an array, join together into a string with an optional separator.

content.text:
```ts
'C E G'.join() // returns 'CEG'
```

```ts
'C E G'.join(',') // returns 'C,E,G'
```

summary.text:
Listen to a MIDI input device, on a channel. 
Record notes and loop them.
Recorded notes persist across code evaluations until cleared.
MIDI input device name or index
MIDI channel to listen on (1–16)

additional arguments for loop control -
cycles: number or pattern - length of the loop in cycles. Default is 1.
record: boolean or pattern - whether to record incoming notes. Default is true.
clear: boolean or pattern - whether to clear recorded notes. Default is false.
Less than comparison. Returns 1 if value < threshold, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
seq(1,2,3).lt(saw(0,4,3)) // compares each value with saw values
```

summary.text: Get the current audio level from a Stream.
Parse a mini pattern string into a Pattern instance.

content.text:
```ts
mini('Cmaj7..?') // parses the mini pattern string into a Pattern
```

summary.text: mini pattern string
Modulo by a value or pattern.
MIDI note number to frequency.
MIDI note number or pattern of MIDI note numbers
Map from one range to another.
Multiply a value or pattern.
Not equal to comparison. Returns 1 if value != threshold, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
seq(1,2,3).neq(saw(0,4,3)) // compares each value with saw values
```

summary.text: Noise waveform from min to max over one cycle.

content.text:
```ts
noise() // noise values between 0 and 1
```

```ts
noise(5, 10) // noise values between 5 and 10
```

```ts
noise().slow(3) // noise values between 0 and 1, slowed down over 3 cycles
```

summary.text: If a value is true, negate it (1 -> 0, 0 -> 1).

content.text:
```ts
coin().not() // returns 1 when coin() is 0, else 0
```

```ts
not(coin()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Return mostly 1s, occasionally 0s.

content.text:
```ts
often()
```

```ts
often('A', 'B') // returns 'A' when condition is true, else 'B'
```

```ts
(60).add(often(5)) // adds 5 to 60 most of the time
```

```ts
(60).add((5).often()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Return a 1 at the earliest convenience, then 0s after that.
Compare values. If one of them is true, return 1, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
coin().or(coin()) // returns 1 when either coin() is true
```

summary.text: Print the current Pattern value for debugging.

content.text:
```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C').print() // prints the value of the pattern on each division of the cycle.
```

```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C').print('MyPattern') // prints the value of the pattern with an identifier.
```

summary.text: Generate a pulse wave from min to max over one cycle.

content.text:
```ts
pulse(0, 1, 0.25) // a pulse wave from 0 to 1 with a duty cycle of 25% over the course of 1 cycle
```

summary.text: minimum value
maximum value
duty cycle (0 to 1)
quantization: steps/cycle. Default 48. Increase for a more fine-grained waveform.
Alias for qmeasure.
Measure Qubit at index.

content.text:
```ts
qm(0) // measures qubit 0
```

summary.text: qubit index
how many times to measure the circuit each cycle. Default is 16ths.
Measure Qubit at index.

content.text:
```ts
qm(0) // measures qubit 0
```

summary.text: qubit index
how many times to measure the circuit each cycle. Default is 16ths.
Measure all Qubits. Returns an array of measured values.

content.text:
```ts
qmeasures() // measures all qubits
```

summary.text: how many times to measure the circuit each cycle. Default is 16ths.
Alias for qmeasures.
Measure all Qubits. Returns an array of measured values.

content.text:
```ts
qmeasures() // measures all qubits
```

summary.text: how many times to measure the circuit each cycle. Default is 16ths.
Alias for qphase.
Phase of basis state at index.

content.text:
```ts
qphase(0) // phase of basis state 0
```

summary.text: basis state index
Phase of basis state at index.

content.text:
```ts
qphase(0) // phase of basis state 0
```

summary.text: basis state index
Phases of all basis states. As an array.

content.text:
```ts
qphases() // phases of all basis states
```

summary.text: Alias for qphases.
Phases of all basis states. As an array.

content.text:
```ts
qphases() // phases of all basis states
```

summary.text: Alias for qprob.
Probability of basis state at index.

content.text:
```ts
qprob(0) // probability of basis state 0
```

summary.text: basis state index
Probability of basis state at index.

content.text:
```ts
qprob(0) // probability of basis state 0
```

summary.text: basis state index
Probabilities of all basis states. As an array.

content.text:

```ts
qprobs() // probabilities of all basis states
```

summary.text: Alias for qprobs.
Probabilities of all basis states. As an array.

content.text:
```ts
qprobs() // probabilities of all basis states
```

summary.text: Alias for saw
Generate a ramp from min to max, once per cycle.

content.text:
```ts
saw(0,4) // a ramp from 0 to 4 over the course of 1 cycle
```

```ts
saw(0,1,96).slow(2) // a ramp from 0 to 1 over the course of 2 cycles, with finer steps to mitigate the slow pattern
```

summary.text: start value
end value
quantization: steps/cycle. Default 48. Increase for a more fine-grained waveform.
Alias for saw
Generate a ramp from min to max, once per cycle.

content.text:
```ts
saw(0,4) // a ramp from 0 to 4 over the course of 1 cycle
```

```ts
saw(0,1,96).slow(2) // a ramp from 0 to 1 over the course of 2 cycles, with finer steps to mitigate the slow pattern
```

summary.text: start value
end value
quantization: steps/cycle. Default 48. Increase for a more fine-grained waveform.
Return mostly 0s, occasionally 1s. Optionally, provide if-else patterns.

content.text:
```ts
rarely()
```

```ts
rarely('A', 'B') // returns 'A' when condition is true, else 'B'
```

```ts
(60).add(rarely(5)) // adds 5 to 60 occasionally
```

```ts
(60).add((5).rarely()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return values from x1,y1 to x2,y2. If x2 or y2 are out of bounds, they will wrap around to the beginning of the row or column.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4).region(0,0,2,2) // returns the top-left 2x2 region of the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

summary.text: Reset a pattern's internal time when a condition is truthy.

content.text:
```ts
sine(0,1).reset(every(0.5)) // sine wave that restarts every half cycle
```

```ts
saw(0,1).reset(coin()) // saw wave that randomly resets
```

summary.text: pattern to evaluate. When truthy, resets the pattern's time origin.
Retrieve data from the store
key of the data to retrieve
optional additional arguments to retrieve nested data.

Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return values in concentric rings from the centre outwards. The first ring (0) is the centre cell, the second ring (1) is the 8 neighbours, the third ring (2) is the 16 cells surrounding those, etc.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4).ring(0) // returns the centre cell of the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

```ts
ca(4).ring(1) // returns the 8 neighbours of the centre cell in the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

summary.text: Shift a pattern in time by n cycles.

content.text:
```ts
sine().rotate(0.5) // sine wave 0.5 cycles out of phase
```

```ts
seq(1,0,1,0).rotate(0.25) // shift sequence by a quarter cycle
```

summary.text: number of cycles to shift. Can be a number or a Pattern.
Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return all values in the given row.

content.text:
```ts
ca(4).row(0) // returns the first row of the 4x4 Game of Life grid
```

```ts
ca(4).row(count()) // returns a different row each event, cycling through the rows as count() increments
```

summary.text: Generate a ramp from min to max, once per cycle.

content.text:
```ts
saw(0,4) // a ramp from 0 to 4 over the course of 1 cycle
```

```ts
saw(0,1,96).slow(2) // a ramp from 0 to 1 over the course of 2 cycles, with finer steps to mitigate the slow pattern
```

summary.text: start value
end value
quantization: steps/cycle. Default 48. Increase for a more fine-grained waveform.
Alias for mtr.
Sequence values into a single cycle.

content.text:
```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C', 'D') // A for .25 cycle, B for .25 cycle ... D for .25 cycle
```

summary.text: Alias for cat.
Concat values, one per cycle.

content.text:
```ts
cat('A', 'B', 'C') // A for 1 cycle, B for 1 cycle, C for 1 cycle.
```

summary.text: Generate a sine wave from min to max over one cycle.

content.text:
```ts
sine(0, 1) // a sine wave from 0 to 1 over the course of 1 cycle
```

```ts
sine(-1, 1, 96).slow(2) // a sine wave from -1 to 1 over the course of 2 cycles, with finer steps to mitigate the slow pattern
```

summary.text: minimum value
maximum value
quantization: steps/cycle. Default 48. Increase for a more fine-grained waveform.
Slow down a pattern.

content.text:
```ts
seq('A', 'B', 'C').slow(2) // A for 2 cycles, B for 2 cycles, C for 2 cycles.
```

summary.text: Alias for coin
Return an equal distribution of 1s and 0s.

content.text:
```ts
coin() // returns 0 or 1 with equal probability
```

```ts
coin('A', 'B') // returns 'A' or 'B' with equal probability
```

```ts
(60).add(coin(5)) // adds either 0 or 5 to 60 with equal probability
```

```ts
(60).add((5).coin()) // same as above
```

summary.text: Alias for square
Alias for pulse with a duty cycle of 0.5.

content.text:
```ts
square(0, 4) // a square wave from 0 to 4 over the course of 1 cycle
```

summary.text: minimum value
maximum value
Alias for pulse with a duty cycle of 0.5.

content.text:
```ts
square(0, 4) // a square wave from 0 to 4 over the course of 1 cycle
```

summary.text: minimum value
maximum value
Layer values over the same time range.

content.text:
```ts
stack(0, sine(), square()) // layers a sine wave and square wave over a constant 0 value.
```

summary.text: Round to a particular step.

content.text:
```ts
random().round(0.25) // random values rounded to nearest 0.25
```

summary.text: Subtract a value or pattern.
Return the current time in cycles.

content.text:
```ts
t() // current time e.g. 3.25 (3 cycles and a quarter)
```

```ts
t(4) // current time modulo 4
```

summary.text: optional modulo value

Assuming an array whose length is a perfect square, treat it as a grid and return values of the specified tile.
Tiles are indexed starting from the top-left, going row by row. For example, in a 4x4 grid, tile(2,2) would return the bottom-right 2x2 section.
Toggle 1s and 0s when the condition is met.

content.text:
```ts
rarely().toggle() // toggles between 1 and 0 each time rarely() returns 1
```

summary.text: pattern to evaluate
Generate a triangle wave from min to max over one cycle

content.text:
```ts
tri(0, 1) // a triangle wave from 0 to 1 over the course of 1 cycle
```

summary.text: minimum value
maximum value
quantization: steps/cycle. Default 48. Increase for a more fine-grained waveform.
Compare values. If one is true and the other is false, return 1, else 0.

content.text:
```ts
set(1).xor(1) // returns 0
```

readme.text:
# Satori
A live coding language for unlimited pattern interference. It supersedes Zen, which was restricted to grid-based event triggering. Satori offers fully flexible timing, and allows parameters from any musical layer to control or transform those of any other. As with Zen, it also includes a simple API for designing, running, and sonifying quantum algorithms via its built-in simulator.

The codebase is modular by design. You can use Satori as a complete live coding environment—combining the core language, editor, console, help docs, and synthesis engine—or import individual modules into your own projects. See the docs below for different use cases. A fully functional version of Satori is hosted at: [satori.cephasteom.co.uk](https://satori.cephasteom.co.uk).

## Local Development
To run this project locally, as a complete application:
* clone this repo
* run
`nvm use`

(node version manager) to change node to correct version
*
`npm i`

to install dependencies
*
`npm run dev`

for hot file reloading
* or
`npm run build`
and
`npm run preview`

to use bundled package

## Using modules

### To use Satori in your own applications
Handlers are functions that process events. Each event has an ID (source), parameters, a trigger time, and a flag indicating whether it is a mutation or a regular event.
You can create custom handlers for Satori to connect to your system, or use Satori’s built-in ones.

### To use standalone synth engine (Oto)
You can use the synth engine directly, without the need to write Satori code. Simply initialise Oto, then send your own events via the handler.
### To use standalone patterning language

```js
import { Pattern, methods } from './core/Pattern'

const p = new Pattern()
console.log(p.sine().query(0,1)) // query pattern between 0 and 1 cycles

const { saw } = methods // if you want to nest, get nested methods from methods object
const p2 = new Pattern()
console.log(p2.coin().fast(8).ifelse(
    saw(0,10),
    saw(10,1)
))
```
### To use the standalone code editor

```js
import { init } from './editor'

init('#editor')

window.addEventListener("evaluateCode", (e) => console.log(e.detail.code));
```
Will load the editor in the element provided by the id. Default is
`#editor`
. Listen out for the
`evaluateCode`

event to handle the editor output.

### To use the standalone console
Initialise the console, passing in the element in which it should render. Default is
`#console`
. Send messages to the console using the BroadcastChannel interface. Types are
`info`
`success`
, and
`error`

.

## SuperSatori
The internal synth engine Oto is convenient, but its use of RNBO results in a fairly convoluted workflow for adding new synths. Satori also has a Supercollider backend, which sounds great, and is more easily extendable. See [https://github.com/cephasteom/supersatori](https://github.com/cephasteom/supersatori) for installation and setup.

Reload Satori, appending
`?engine=supersatori`
to the url. If successful, you should see
`Supersatori connected`

printed to the console.

## External control via websockets
Incoming and outgoing traffic can be handled by websockets. Use cases:
* You want to send code to Satori from an external source
* You want to send datasets into Satori from an external source
* You want to listen for code changes in Satori and consume them elsewhere

Reload Satori, appending
`?ws=true&wsPort=<port>`
to the url. This instantiates a web socket server on
`http://localhost:<port>`

. The port parameter is optional and defaults to 5001. 

On evaluate it will broadcast:
See
`src/core/WebSocket.ts`

for handing of messages in.

Run
`node src/tests/websockets.cjs`

to test the round trip.

## Acknowledgements
* This series of blog posts by Froos helped me finally crack time: [garten.salat.dev](https://garten.salat.dev/idlecycles/).
* Code editor built with: [Prism](https://github.com/jonpyt/prism-code-editor/?tab=readme-ov-file).
* Mini-language built on: [PegJS](https://github.com/pegjs/pegjs).
* Quantum simulator built with: [Quantum Circuit](https://www.npmjs.com/package/quantum-circuit).
